{"key_event":"1833 Constitution centralizes Portalian conservative state","reasoning":"By 1837 Chile had moved from post-independence instability toward a centralized presidential regime with stronger fiscal resources, civilian control of the army, and mobilization capacity, despite temporary disruption from civil war and mutiny. Civil society gained wealth among commercial and mining elites, but broader liberal, regional, and popular capacity to check the state narrowed under conservative-oligarchic dominance and restricted electoral rules.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":3.5,"state_change":1.5,"state_power":6.5}
